summary: iter240l-gamefix — Admin User Detail game_activity rebuilt from `transactions`. Verified backend payload shape and business-value thresholds for both target users, plus frontend GamesTab UI/integration via Admin modal.
critical_code_review_comments: admin_user_detail.py is now ~430 lines — fine for now but trending toward the 700-line soft limit; consider splitting into separate router files (notes / restrictions / detail) if more endpoints are added.

The 'last_played_at' field in quiz is computed from quiz_plays which includes 'quiz_challenge_bonus' (PTS rewards). This is correct given the design, but be aware it can shift forward even on a pure-PTS bonus event (no real bet locked).

All_transaction_types includes types like 'send' / 'referral_bonus' that are not games. That is intentional (admin debug breakdown), but the i18n label 'debug_tx_breakdown' should make this clear in every locale — verified for FR; please spot-check EN/ES/AR/RU.
The frontend GamesTab keeps the previous user's data visible for ~1s during the API reload when switching from one user to another (no skeleton). Minor UX nit; not blocking.
seed_data_creation: None — used real prod-like data for users usr_mig_3d84c1905fe5 (@JMGHOMSI) and user_7d622b644231 (@balebasimb04da)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_admin_user_detail_iter240l.py covers (1) admin-auth gating, (2) full schema of game_activity (quiz / fortune_wheel / mini_spin / staking / all_transaction_types), (3) business thresholds for both target users, (4) no-regression on other dossier sections. Preview URL has ~30s cold-start — tests have a warmup fixture. Frontend selectors: data-testid='view-user-<user_id>' on AdminPage user row; modal data-testid='admin-user-detail-modal'; tab buttons 'aud-tab-<id>'; quiz values 'aud-quiz-played' / 'aud-quiz-won-usd' / 'aud-quiz-won-pts'; debug 'aud-tx-debug' (collapsible) and 'aud-tx-debug-table'. Verified live: BALE played=11, won_usd=36, won_pts=100, refunded=32, debug rows=5.
rca of the issue: Not applicable — bug was a feature fix delivered by E1; testing confirmed it works as expected.
